principles.label: The Immaterial Nature of True Beauty
principles.summary: True philosophical love and devotion must be directed toward the immaterial 'Beautiful' rather than the physical human body, which is tied to base and unclean realities.
principles.details: Rooted in Neoplatonic thought, physical passion is viewed as a base distraction from higher intellectual and spiritual pursuits. Hypatia maintained determined celibacy and actively repelled physical lust to emphasize this principle.
principles.representative_quote: It is this you love, young man, not beauty

principles.label: Mathematics as the Foundation of Truth
principles.summary: Geometry and arithmetic provide the rigorous, logical foundation required to understand the harmonious structure of the universe and access divine truths.
principles.details: Hypatia viewed mathematical structure and harmony not merely as secular tools, but as fixed standards of truth and a direct reflection of the divine order.
principles.representative_quote: ...it proceeds to its demonstrations in no uncertain way, for it uses as its servants geometry and arithmetic, which it would not be improper to call a fixed standard of truth.

principles.label: Astronomy as a Pathway to Theology
principles.summary: The rational motion of heavenly bodies imitates the divine mind, making the physical study of the heavens a necessary passage to understanding esoteric metaphysics.
principles.details: While heavenly bodies are material, their predictable and rational movements reflect a higher divine intelligence, bridging the gap between the physical world and ineffable theology.
principles.representative_quote: Astronomy itself is a venerable science, and might become a stepping stone to something more august, a science which I think is a convenient passage to ineffeable theology...

principles.label: Emanation from the One
principles.summary: All of existence emanates from a single, ultimate, and divine source known as the One.
principles.details: This is the foundational concept of the Neoplatonic school of philosophy, which Hypatia adhered to and publicly taught to her followers in Alexandria.

principles.label: The Esoteric Nature of Deep Philosophy
principles.summary: The deepest mysteries of philosophy require an elevated understanding that is not readily accessible to the general public.
principles.details: Despite her practice of giving public lectures and acting as a secular advisor, Hypatia maintained the Neoplatonic view that true philosophical enlightenment requires a level of intellectual rigor that the masses generally lack.

mental_models.label: The Hierarchy of Knowledge
mental_models.summary: A progressive educational and philosophical model where foundational sciences support observational sciences, which in turn serve as the gateway to the highest level of spiritual understanding.
mental_models.details: In this model, geometry and arithmetic form the base. They enable astronomy (the observation of the heavens), which ultimately leads the mind to comprehend ineffable theology and the divine mind.
mental_models.representative_quote: Astronomy itself is a venerable science, and might become a stepping stone to something more august...

heuristics.label: Confronting Base Desires with Physical Reality
heuristics.summary: Using stark, shocking demonstrations of the physical body's base nature to jolt individuals out of superficial lust and redirect their focus toward higher intellectual pursuits.
heuristics.details: Famously demonstrated when Hypatia threw a soiled menstrual rag at a student who had fallen in love with her, using the shock of physical uncleanliness to cure him of his passion and return him to his 'right mind'.
heuristics.representative_quote: This is what you are in love with, young man, and not with the Beautiful

signature_quotes.label: On the Illusion of Physical Beauty
signature_quotes.summary: Spoken to a student who had fallen in love with her, while throwing a menstrual rag at him to demonstrate the unclean nature of the physical body.
signature_quotes.details: This quote survives in various historical fragments as a testament to her strict adherence to Neoplatonic ideals over physical desires.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: It is this you love, young man, not beauty

signature_quotes.label: On Astronomy as a Stepping Stone
signature_quotes.summary: Synesius describing the philosophical framework taught by Hypatia, enclosed with the gift of an astrolabe.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: Astronomy itself is a venerable science, and might become a stepping stone to something more august, a science which I think is a convenient passage to ineffeable theology...

signature_quotes.label: On the Heavens Imitating the Mind
signature_quotes.summary: Explaining why the physical study of the heavens connects to the divine mind.
signature_quotes.representative_quote: ...for the happy body of heaven has matter underneath it, and its motion has seemed to the leaders in philosophy to be an imitation of mind.

anti_patterns.label: Confusing Physical Lust with True Beauty
anti_patterns.summary: Pursuing physical passion for the human body under the guise of loving beauty.
anti_patterns.details: This fails because physical bodies are tied to base, unbeautiful realities. True philosophical love should be directed toward higher, non-physical beauty. Succumbing to lust distracts the mind from the pursuit of higher culture and the immaterial 'Beautiful'.
anti_patterns.representative_quote: This is what you are in love with, young man, and not with the Beautiful
